title: Brazil Approves First Semaglutide Pen as EMS Challenges Novo Nordisk's Ozempic
summary: Sao Paulo: Brazil's health ‚Äãregulator Anvisa said on ‚ÄãTuesday it had approved EMS's ‚ÄãOzivy, the country's first semaglutide-based injector pen, as the drugmaker looks to expand into a higher-end...

contentHtml:
Sao Paulo: Brazil's health ‚Äãregulator Anvisa said on ‚ÄãTuesday it had approved EMS's ‚ÄãOzivy, the country's first semaglutide-based injector pen, as the drugmaker looks to expand into a higher-end ‚Äåsegment of ‚ÅÝthe ‚ÅÝglobal pharmaceutical industry.

The drug uses the same active ingredient as Novo Nordisk's diabetes and weight-loss drug Ozempic, whose patent expired in Brazil in March.

Drug to be produced at ‚ÄãEMS plant in Sao ‚ÅÝPaulo state ‚Äåwith capacity for up to ‚Äã40 ‚Äãmillion pens per year.

CEO ‚ÅÝof Grupo NC, which controls EMS, told Reuters in March the firm expected to launch semaglutide pens this year.

Following the registration, the drug will be marketed once Brazil's medicine pricing chamber approves a price cap.

For inclusion in Brazil's public healthcare system SUS, Ozivy must be ‚Äãapproved ‚Äãby the ‚ÅÝHealth Ministry.

Five other synthetic semaglutide applications and one biological version are under review by ‚ÄãAnvisa.

EMS rival Hypera had previously said it also planned to launch its generic version of semaglutide this year.

Source : Reuters

Sheeba Farhat Joined Medical Dialogues in 2018 to report on the latest Education news. A Graduate of the University of Delhi, she specializes in covering stories related to Medical Education updates. For inquiries or further information, you can reach her at editorial@medicaldialogues.in.
